project: i-Prime AGV System — HMI Design Spec (Goodyear)

agvs.note: all AGVs serve all lines; no dedicated assignment

calling_stations.semantic: button press = call + lorry variant in one event

home_station_pc.process: iprime_master (asyncio): Modbus poll + dispatcher + MQTT client + Flask/WS + sqlite logger

system.control_flow: DI rising edge → Call object in queue → dispatcher assigns first idle AGV → MQTT dispatch cmd → AGV ack + execute. HMI is read-only view; never a peer.

dispatcher.agv_selection_note: no distance logic; first AGV with state=IDLE at home gets assigned
dispatcher.supermarket_flow: AGV parks at home; supermarket operator loads correct lorry variant; operator presses Depart to release AGV

home_hmi.tech: Flask + Vue3 CDN (no build step) or htmx; WebSocket state push at 10 Hz or on-change; no auth
home_hmi.touch: min 12mm×12mm (~85px) targets; min 18px body text; assume gloves

1_operations.layout: left: next-action panel + three AGV status cards; right: single call queue (all lines, all AGVs) + alarm strip; persistent bottom nav 80px

next_action_panel.content: lorry variant (SMALL/BIG, large font, colour-coded) + target AGV + destination line + DEPART button
next_action_panel.depart_enabled_when: AGV at home (RFID HOME tag + AGV at_home=true via MQTT)

1_operations.call_queue_row: line_id | lorry_variant | age mm:ss | state | assigned_agv
1_operations.long_press_row: contextual menu: Cancel / Force-assign to specific AGV

agv_cards.fields: mode (colour-coded) + zone/line + battery (bar, warn@30%, alarm@15%) + speed + current task

1_operations.alarm_strip: active alarms only; tap to silence buzzer; visual clears on condition clear; icons: ⚠warn ✕fault ⓘinfo

2_manual_dispatch.layout: single-page left-to-right wizard: 1.AGV (any of 3) → 2.Destination (10-button grid, all lines always enabled) → 3.Variant (SMALL/BIG) → 4.DISPATCH button
2_manual_dispatch.validation: exactly 1 destination required; variant required; any AGV selectable
2_manual_dispatch.abort_section: shows active dispatches per AGV + [CANCEL DISPATCH] [RECALL HOME] per AGV
2_manual_dispatch.agv_control: [FORCE TO MANUAL] (requires physical switch) | [RETURN HOME] per AGV

3_diagnostics_kpis.left: shift cycle counts total + per-line breakdown; lorry variant histogram (SMALL vs BIG); AGV uptime/availability%; system health (Modbus module, MQTT, dispatcher loop ms)
3_diagnostics_kpis.right: AGV telemetry selector (AGV-1/2/3); full AMRState snapshot; alarm history last 50 events
3_diagnostics_kpis.data_source: sqlite event log, per-shift, no MES/ERP integration v1

home_hmi.andon: 3-colour tower (green/amber/red) + buzzer; wired via DO on Modbus bus; dispatcher writes on highest alarm level

agv_hmi.runtime: Chromium-kiosk on AGV PC (Ubuntu 22); Flask app reading AMRState via Unix socket or local MQTT topic
agv_hmi.normal_use: physical pendant + station buttons; HMI for maintenance/commissioning/troubleshooting only

pages.1_status: mode indicator; tape/lateral-error gauge; last RFID; current task+stage; battery bar+voltage; L/R wheel RPM; PID output; link health (CAN, Modbus DIO, Modbus AO, RFID, MQTT, pendant); active alarms

2_manual_control.gate: physical mode switch = MANUAL AND (pendant detected OR Maintenance Mode toggle active)
2_manual_control.jog: 8-direction momentary buttons; pusher UP/DOWN/TOGGLE (conditional on hardware); manual speed V_MAN configurable
2_manual_control.safety: WebSocket heartbeat; >200ms silence → control stack forces idle

pages.3_parameters: read-only by default; Edit gated by Maintenance Mode; PID gains per speed mode; speed targets; accel ramp; V_RED coefficients; RFID command map; saves to parameters.json + soft-reload
pages.4_io_alarms: live DI/DO bits + AO voltages + raw CAN frames + last RFID; labelled by system_summary.json symbolic names; alarm log last 100 events; CSV export

comms.dispatch_id: UUID per call; echoed on every AGV ack/event for dedup/ordering

transitions: any AGV idle at home → ASSIGNED (first-idle-wins)
AGV idle at home → AT_HOME_LOAD (next-action shown to supermarket operator)
operator loads lorry + presses Depart → EN_ROUTE
line operator swaps lorry + presses depart → RETURNING
call_lifecycle.logging: every transition timestamped to sqlite; raw data for KPI cycle counts and timing

tech_stack.frontend: Vue3 CDN or htmx + vanilla JS (no Node toolchain on production PC)
tech_stack.ipc: WebSocket (websockets lib) for HMI; MQTT for AGVs

igp_scope: 1 main/home station + 10 call stations (2 buttons each)
customer_scope: Trolley hook modification per AGV spec
Track area conditioning (turning radii, forklift clearance)

removed: 228 DI inputs → 20 DI inputs (~8 Modbus modules → 1)
Multi-stop dispatch logic for zone B/C
changed: Single unified call queue (was 2 per loop)
Manual dispatch: destination grid 10 lines flat (was loop-filtered 38)
dispatch payload: destination (singular) + lorry_variant (was stops[] + variant int)
unchanged: Home station PC architecture and process model
MQTT topic schema and QoS strategy
Call lifecycle pattern (button → queue → assign → depart → line → return)
Alarm matrix and Andon tower logic

open_questions: VMI-6 not present in Goodyear slide (only VMI-1 to VMI-5 + 4x R2.5 = 9 lines). Confirm VMI-6 is real or adjust LINES array to 9.
All 3 AGVs share one home/supermarket position, or are there multiple departure bays?
If 2 AGVs are idle simultaneously and a call arrives, first-idle-wins requires a deterministic tiebreak — suggest AGV-ID ascending order.
Pusher mechanism: present on Goodyear deployment units? Affects AGV HMI Page 2.
Calling-station LED feedback: 20 indicator lamps feasible (much smaller than 228). Decision before hardware procurement.
10.4" display confirmed for Goodyear home station, or different size?
